demos.subject: 📬 Your Student News | Term 2 Week 9 2025
demos.body:
Tell us how your term is going 😊
For a better experience, view your news online
Follow us on Insta
The myExperience survey is now open! 📢
Take a few minutes to share your thoughts on your Term 2 courses and help shape the future of education at UNSW.
Complete it on Moodle
UNSW is located on the unceded territory of the Bedegal (Kensington campus), Gadigal (City and Paddington campuses) and Ngunnawal peoples (UNSW Canberra) who are the Traditional Owners of the lands where each campus of UNSW is situated.
Read on
.
In this week's Student News, protect yourself from
phishing
, discover
wellness tips
, sign up for
Science Week workshops
and find out where to get
free period products
!
The Official Stuff
Eduroam WiFi rollout at Kensington Campus 🛜
Uniwide shuts down on Fri, 1 Aug
Our old WiFi network is being replaced at UNSW. To continue your internet access,
connect your device
.
Having issues connecting?
Contact IT
.
Last chance to complete our survey and win a voucher! 🤑
Survey closes Mon, 28 Jul, 11.59pm
Help us improve the way we share info with you! Share your thoughts on our emails, newsletters, Instagram and website. You'll go into the draw to win 1 of 10 $30 vouchers.
Complete the survey
.
Unwell and got an assessment due soon? 🤒
When illness or other circumstances beyond your control affect your ability to undertake assessments, you may be eligible to apply
for
Special Consideration
.
Get vaccinated for Hepatitis on campus 💉
Hepatitis is a blood-borne virus that can cause liver inflammation and some people are more at risk than others. Learn where to get your Hepatitis A and B immunisations on campus.
See more
.
Beware of phishing scams 🎣
Phishing emails use urgency to scare you into sharing personal information. Stay safe by checking that URLs use official domains, reporting dodgy messages as phishing and deleting suspicious messages.
Learn more
.
Protect your vehicles and belongings 🚨
There's been a recent increase in both car and motorcycle thefts. Ensure that your vehicles are insured and registered, always lock them and avoid leaving valuables inside. Report any suspicious behaviour to UNSW Security.
Learn more
.
We're improving the student experience at UNSW 💛
The Student Experience Program is a University-wide initiative created for you, based on what you’ve told us matters most! We’re working closely with students who are sharing their feedback and ideas to help shape the program and create meaningful change.
We’re working towards:
Better digital tools and platforms that make it easier to manage your studies.
Smarter assessments and feedback that help you learn and grow.
More moments for connection with peers, faculty and the wider UNSW community.
Simpler degree programs.
Learn more
Employability
Leadership Foundations September Intensive 💪
Apps close Sun, 3 Aug
Join a two-day leadership program held on campus. Adopt a growth mindset, build resilience and grit and drive team success.
Apply today
.
Looking for graduate opportunities or internships?
Tue, 29 Jul until Fri, 5 Aug
Don't miss Meet The Employers, a free online internship and graduate careers event hosted by GradConnection! Connect directly with top graduate employers!
Register now
.
2025 UNSW Employability Award Night winners 🥇
Congrats to the top 20 winners! They were selected for their exceptional performance across application, psychometric testing, video interview and assessment centre skills.
Opportunities
Learn about generative AI in art 🎨
Explore the intersection of creativity and technology in Generative AI for Artists (DART2252), a new elective for Term 3. Learn how to use AI tools and critically examine their impact on the creative industries.
Learn more
.
UNSW Founders New Wave Program 🌟
Mon, 1 Sep until Fri, 12 Sep, MCIC
The New Wave Program helps women build entrepreneurial skills and confidence. It's the perfect opportunity to upskill in a fun and supportive environment.
Learn more
.
Apply for the Westpac Future Leaders Scholarship 💸
Apps close on Wed, 27 Aug, 5pm
Interested in postgraduate studies in 2026? You could get
$120,000
for coursework or research, along with networking and leadership development opportunities.
Explore the scholarship
.
Get rewarded for completing student research surveys! 🤑
Complete surveys run by UNSW research students to help them with their theses! Each survey has its own eligibility criteria and completion incentives.
See more
.
Nominate a teacher for your chance to win a $100 voucher!
Nominations close Sun, 17 Aug
Nominate a staff member who's had an impact on you for the 2025 Students’ Choice Teaching Award! If you write a winning nomination, you will receive a $100 gift voucher!
Nominate now
.
Interested in intercultural mentorship and leadership?
Apps close Monday, 4 Aug
Become a
Global Connections
mentor or English communication facilitator in T3! Develop leadership skills, support your peers and get AHEGS recognition
!
Apply now
.
From your Faculty
Your Faculty Noticeboard
See your faculty's latest news, opportunities and events.
Check it out
Support
Student study tip of the week 💡
"Use the ARC term planner (or create your own) to write down all key dates for the term—such as assignment deadlines, exams, and events—and keep it somewhere visible. This helps you stay on top of upcoming tasks and plan ahead. Pair this with the ARC weekly planner to break down your goals into daily tasks.
The limited space on the weekly planner prevents overloading your schedule, helping you stay realistic and avoid overwhelm. It’s also motivating to tick off completed tasks and visually track your progress.
Extra Tip: Review your planner at the start of each week to adjust your plan based on new priorities or unexpected changes."
- Prisha (Bachelor of Software Engineering)
Submit a tip
If we publish your tip in Student News this term, you'll get a
$5 voucher
to
The Little Marionette
. 💸
Your weekly Study Hacks Workshops ✅
This week, learn how to write a great essay, brush up on ref [ORDER_ID] and prepare for exams with smart study tips
.
Sign up now
.
Improve your academic language with mySkillSearch
Get a personalised map of resources to enhance your writing, speaking, reading and listening skills.
Try it now
.
Thinking about exchange? 🌏
Join us at the Exchange Application Assistance Session to learn how to apply for an exchange program!
Sign up
.
Majoring in stress? 😩
We partner with TalkCampus, an online student wellbeing app. It's your space to anonymously vent, get advice or share the tea on what's going on in your world with other students.
Check it out
.
Counsellors share their wellness tips ❤️‍🩹
We asked counsellors from
Psychology and Wellness
what their top wellness tips are. Here's what they said:
“Breathe intentionally - take a few minutes each day to do deep breathing - inhale for 4 seconds, hold for 4 seconds and exhale for 4. It calms the nervous system quickly.”
“Intentionally unplug and step away from your screens, especially social media, even if it’s just 1 hour before you sleep.”
“I love to get out into the sun. It’s a natural mood booster.”
Events
Open Studio - Everything is Connected workshop 🎨
Wed, 30 Jul, 12.30 - 2.30pm, Main Library
Create a unique hybrid collage then bring it to life using stop-motion animation!
Register today
.
Scholarly AI resources for students & researchers 💻
Wed, 30 Jul & Mon, 4 Aug, Main Library
Learn how to use generative AI-powered search tools like Scite.AI and Scopus AI
for research and learning.
See more
.
Science Week: Materials Revolution MICROfactorie tour
Tue, 12 Aug, 1 - 2.15pm, Main Library
Join us for a guided tour of the Library exhibition "Materials Revolution", and a behind-the-scenes tour of UNSW SMaRT Centre’s MICROfactorie Laboratory.
Register now
.
Science Week: Plastic Micro-Recycling workshops ♻️
Thu, 14 Aug, 12 - 12.45pm & 1.30 - 2.15pm, Main Library
Join these workshops led by Defy Designs, a company that specialises in designing and manufacturing beautiful products made from plastic destined for landfill.
Register now
.
Arc Fun!
Check out the Food Hub 🍎
The Food Hub is a free food pantry on campus. Stop by to pick up nutritious food to fuel your studies! You must register in advance, plus don't forget to bring your containers!
Learn more
.
Grab a free breakfast 🌅
Every Tue, Wed and Thu, 9.30am - 12.30pm
Mornings can be tough, but a free breakfast can help. Find Brekkie Club by the Basser Steps and set yourself up for success with a dose of mindful ref [ORDER_ID].
Find out more
.
Looking for more Arc fun? 🫶
There are loads of events and activities taking place weekly on campus.
Check it out
Choose to Reuse
Forgot your reusables? Here’s how to still cut waste on campus ☕
Left your cup or container at home? Don’t worry, there are still smart, sustainable choices you can make! Try dining in
using reusable crockery at campus cafés or go lid-free to reduce single-use waste.
These small swaps help cut down on packaging waste and limit exposure to chemicals found in disposable containers.
Did you know? If you lined up all the disposable cups used at UNSW in a year, they’d cover the entire Library Lawn! 😱
Explore the Choose to Reuse Challenge
Sustainability tips of the week 💡
"Instead of wrapping gifts in wrapping paper, which gets thrown out straight away, I like to get creative and wrap gifts in nice fabrics or scarves that the recipient can reuse to reduce waste from gift wrapping (and it becomes a fun second gift!)"
- Nomi (Bachelor of Engineering/Science)
"Keep all clean plastic bags (especially from delivery) and reuse them as trash liners instead of buying new ones."
- Luthfia (Master of Education)
Submit a tip
If we publish your tip in Student News this term, you'll get a
$5 voucher
to
The Little Marionette
. 💸
There's still time to enter the BYO photo comp
🌱
We’re in the last weeks of the campaign, so keep those BYO cup and container photos coming! You’re not just in the running for prizes, you’re helping make UNSW more sustainable with every post.
Enter now
.
Everything Else!
Skip the food and drink queues! ⏩
Craving a hot brew or snack on campus but don't want to wait in line? Order [ORDER_ID] from
XS Espresso
,
Guzman y Gomez
,
The Little Marionette
and
Plume
.
Free period products! 🤩
Did you know that you can access free period products on campus at UNSW Kensington? Check out the updated period product dispenser map to find your nearest location.
Check it out
.
And finally...
Don't fall for the trap of contract cheating! ⚠️
Contract cheating is submitting someone else's work as your own. It's a big deal and has serious consequences. If you're struggling academically, there is plenty of support available.
Avoid contract cheating
Back to top ^
UNSW is located on the unceded territory of the Bedegal/Bidjigal (Kensington campus), Gadigal (City and Paddington Campuses) and Ngunnawal peoples (UNSW Canberra) who are the Traditional Owners of the lands where each campus of UNSW is situated.
We acknowledge all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Elders, past, present, and emerging, and their communities, who have shared and practised their teachings over thousands of years.
We support the
Uluru Statement from the Heart
.

demos.subject: Learn how to protect yourself against scams
demos.body:
Stay vigilant and report suspicious activity
Having trouble viewing this message?
View it online
instead!
Matthew, m
ost students don't think they will fall for a scam but unfortunately, many do.
Scammers are pretending to be UNSW and attempting to trick students into handing over money or personal details. Some UNSW students have lost thousands of dollars to scammers.
UNSW will never ask you to pay your tuition fees over the phone, on social media or by direct bank transfer.
Authentic emails from UNSW regarding your tuition fees will:
Direct you to pay fees on myUNSW and not through personal bank accounts
Come from an email address ending in @unsw.edu.au.
If you suspect a scam, there are steps you can take to protect yourself, your family and other students:
1. Keep yourself safe
Do not provide any information or money. Hang up any suspicious phone calls, delete suspicious emails and report suspected scams to
UNSW Protective Services
.
2. Report and get support
Contact UNSW Protective Services for guidance. If you have been targeted, please seek support from
Psychology and Wellness
or a
Student Support Advisor
.
Discover the
different types of scams
targeting UNSW students and how to spot them, and don't be afraid to seek help or advice.
Learn more about scams and what to do if you're targeted
If it doesn't feel right, report it
If you're unsure whether a message is from UNSW or a scammer, contact
The Nucleus: Student Hub
for confirmation.
For support or guidance on further actions, contact UNSW Protective Services.
Contact UNSW Protective Services
Back to top
^
Looking for help?
For advice on all things UNSW, including faculty specific questions, essential services, support and development, reach out to the Hub!
Contact The Nucleus: Student Hub

demos.subject: GENC[PHONE]_00696: Reminder: Learning Community reminder – There are 3 things you need so submit
demos.body:
GENC[PHONE]_00696
»
Forums
»
Announcements
»
Reminder: Learning Community reminder – There are 3 things you need so submit
Reminder: Learning Community reminder – There are 3 things you need so submit
by
Andrew Hingston
- Wednesday, 11 March 2026, 10:42 AM
The
Learning Community Assignment
is
due
1pm
this
Thursday 12 Mar 2026
(Week
4
)
.
There
are
three
things that you need to submit:
1. Post a summary (and discussion of the practical applications) on an article
under the closest forum topic under the ‘Learning Community’ section of the
course website.
2. Provide the details of the article under the ‘Learning Community Details’
link under the ‘Learning Community Submission’ section of the course website.
This includes author, title, URL etc.
3. Copy and paste the text of your forum post AND a screenshot of the post into
the Learning Community MS Word Template and submit it under the ‘Learning
Community Report’ link.
The post (1) is for the benefit of your fellow students. Providing the details
(2) allows us to check whether the article that you chose is unique. Copying
and pasting your post into the MS Word template and submitting it (3) is what
is used for both plagiarism detection and grading.
Note that only doing step 1 doesn’t constitute submission. You need to do all
three!
See this post in context

demos.subject: [COMP3331] Notice: Week 10: Final Week !!!
demos.body:
Wen Hu created a notice COMP3331 on Fri 08 November 2024 at 05:34:35 PM.
Week 10: Final Week !!!
Here are a few important announcements for Week 10, which will be the last teaching week of this term:
In the lectures, we will wrap up the Link layer, which we started last week and then move on to Wireless Network. The lecture slides are
available
.
Lab 5
will be due on 12/11/2024 (Tuesday) 10am.This week,
a tutorial
will replace the lab (in your usual labs). This will serve as useful practice for the final exam. Please look through the questions before hand and try to solve them by yourself. All questions on the tutorial are from past exams. So effectively, it is a sample exam.
Please complete the
myExperience surveys
. The response rate so far is very low (e.g., ~6%), and we would appreciate your feedback very much.
The
final exam
will be held
on campus
via
Inspera
(invigilated)
. We have made available several resources for the final exam, including a practice exam. The final exam will be held at:
COMP9331: 1:45pm to 4pm, 30/11/24 (Sat),
COMP3331: 9:45am to 12pm, 02/12/24 (Mon),
You can view and reply to the message by
clicking here.
Reply to this email to contact Wen Hu (
[EMAIL]
).

demos.subject: Learn how to protect yourself against scams 🔒
demos.body:
Stay vigilant and report suspicious activity
Having trouble viewing this message?
View it online
instead!
Matthew, do you know how to spot a scam?
Scammers can strike when you least expect them, tricking you into handing over money or personal details. Scammers are even pretending to be UNSW, resulting in some students losing thousands of dollars.
Take a minute to learn how to identify and respond to scams.
Recognise the red flags
🚩
It could be a scam if you're asked to pay your tuition fees:
Over the phone
On social media
By direct bank transfer
UNSW will never ask you to pay your fees via any method other than myUNSW.
Be aware that any person or service promising a discount on your fees is attempting to scam you and take your money.
Authentic emails from UNSW will:
Direct you to pay tuition fees on myUNSW  ✅
Come from an email address ending in @unsw.edu.au  ✅
Suspect a scam?
Take these steps to protect yourself, your family and other students.
1. Stop
Do not provide any information or money.
Hang up suspicious phone calls.
Do not click links in suspicious emails.
You can verify that a message has come from UNSW and not a scammer by contacting
The Nucleus: Student Hub
.
2. Report
Report suspected scams to
UNSW Protective Services
. Don't be afraid to report or seek advice. Speaking up protects you, your family and other students.
3. Get support
If you have been targeted, contact
Psychology and Wellness
or a
Student Support Advisor
.
Discover the types of scams that target UNSW students
Learn more about how to spot these scams, and what to do if you're targeted.
Learn more

demos.subject: Explore your career potential at UNSW
demos.body:
Enhance your potential at UNSW through personalised guidance from a dedicated career coach via our
Career Explorer Program
.
This is your opportunity to embark on a transformative roadmap to employability, expanding your possibilities and unlocking countless future opportunities in an evolving world.
The program consists of three in-person or online (your choice) coaching sessions:
Session One – group session (45 mins)
Session Two – individual session (30 mins)
Session Three – individual session (30 mins)
Please note
: if you have completed the Career Discovery Coaching Program you will not need to do the
Career Explorer Program
, as it is the same program with a new title.
Initial group session times are now available for Term 2.
Book initial session
Power up your career
We are delivering several practical
Career Seminars
at the beginning of Term Two, open to all UNSW students.
Workshop topics include Creating your resume, Job search strategies, Preparing for interviews, Networking and Recruitment assessments.
View session dates
Jobs spotlight:
SIG - graduate roles and internships
IMC Trading - internships
PSM - graduate and internship roles
ServiceNow - Intern Marketing Associate
The Trade Desk - Software Engineering Intern
Visit UNSWConnect
to view thousands of other opportunities.
UNSW Advantage highlight
Student Minds
– an AHEGS-recognised,
UNSW Advantage
initiative that assists student mental health.
See more
Creating Your Career
Discover your employability strengths, identify skills that need development and earn 6UOC towards your degree via CDEV2000.
Learn more
Congratulations Lauren!
Congratulations to the recent graduates of UNSW Sydney, who are celebrating their official graduation, with ceremonies continuing throughout May. Special mention to Lauren, featured in our Employability cover story just last month!
View Lauren's story
Follow Us:

Ground Floor
John Goodsell Building (f20)
UNSW Sydney 2052
[EMAIL]
Visit Us
View in browser

demos.subject: Apply for T3's Career Discovery Mentoring Program!
demos.body:
Shape the future you want
Qingbin, expand your professional network, gain real-world insights, and explore potential career paths with guidance from an industry mentor in Term 3’s
Career Discovery Mentoring Program
.
This free, five-hour program is delivered either in-person or online and runs from Week 3 to Week 10.
Build confidence in networking, both personally and professionally, and earn AHEGS recognition, to help strengthen your job applications.
Apply here
Navigate your career with confidence
Available in Term 3 and Summer Term, CDEV2000: Creating Your Career is a 6 UOC undergraduate course designed to help you critically ref [ORDER_ID] on your employability, explore future career opportunities, and develop resume and cover letter templates.
View course outline
Jobs spotlight:
Deloitte
Advancy Australia
NSW Government
Mirvac
ANZ
Head to
UNSWConnect
to view thousands of other local and international jobs, graduate roles and internships.
Transferable skills contribute to career success
UNSW alumna and KPMG Director Remy Crick’s career path proves that success is built on more than a degree. It’s the transferable skills – adaptability, curiosity, resilience – that truly set you apart.
Find out more

demos.body:

»

Homework 1 Results + Revision Process
Homework 1 Results + Revision Process
by
Omar Al-Ghattas
- Monday, 31 March 2025, 9:14 AM
Hi all, HW1 has now been graded and results are available on moodle. You can see a breakdown of your grade by clicking on your submission on moodle.
I've also released template solutions.
There is a review period (a link to the review form is available on moodle). If you have any grading issues, please fill out this form and I will get back to you. You should only fill this out after you have compared your ansewrs to the template, and you need to give a good reason for why you think your current grade is incorrect. The review period is open until this
Friday 4th April at 5pm.
Please do not email/contact us directly about grading issues - all such issues must go through the review process.
Thanks!
Omar

demos.subject: [COMP4920] Notice: Second Essay Mark Release
demos.body:
Sebastian Sequoiah-Grayson created a notice COMP4920 on Mon 12 May 2025 at 08:58:04 AM.
Second Essay Mark Release
Hi everyone!
The marks for your Second Essays will become available on Moodle from 9am today.
If your mark is not there then you are either one of the people taking the supp or you are one of the boarder-line cases. The boarder-line cases are those for which Edward and Philip and I shall be meeting this week in order to go over things carefully and reach consensus. There are not many but I do not want anyone to panic of course : )
Congratulations everyone on your hard work!
All best,
Se

demos.subject: Atlassian X DataSoc Datathon 2025
demos.body:
Calling all data, machine learning, and AI enthusiasts — as well as the creatives and innovators of tomorrow.
Have you ever wondered what it’s like to work as a Data Scientist or ML Engineer at Atlassian?
Introducing the
Atlassian X DataSoc Datathon 2025
— your opportunity to explore the world of Atlassian data science and solve real product challenges using data and AI.
What’s a Datathon?
Think hackathon, but focused on data. You’ll receive a rich, synthetic dataset modeled on Atlassian products (Jira, Confluence, Trello, Bitbucket), a real-world challenge, and a few days to uncover insights, develop solutions, and pitch your ideas to a panel
of judges. It’s all about creativity, teamwork, and clear communication.
Why You Should Join:
Work with a sandbox of Atlassian’s ecosystem: users, sessions, events, billing data, and more
Tackle challenges like predicting churn, modeling adoption paths, or building AI-driven recommendations
Build your portfolio with a real-world project
Network with Atlassian industry professionals
Compete for a personalised resume workshop with an Atlassian recruiter — plus other exciting prizes
Key Dates:
Final Presentations:
Tuesday, September 16th at the Atlassian Office
Sign-Up Deadline:
Thursday, 11th  September 2025
